topic: Definition of Relation and Function, Domain, Range, and Notations

questions.question: A doctor prescribes $2$ pills per $10$ pounds of body weight. If $f(x)$ represents the number of pills prescribed for a patient weighing $x$ pounds, what is the value of $f(30)$?

options.A: A relation with two outputs for one input
options.B: A relation where each input has a unique output
options.C: A relation where each output has a unique input

questions.question: What is the domain of the function $f(x) = \frac{1}{x}$?
A researcher studies the growth of bacteria in a controlled environment. The population $P$ after $t$ hours is modeled by $P(t) = 200 \cdot 2^{0.5t}$. What is $P(4)$?
The vertical line test is used to determine if a relation is a
The range of the function $f(x) = |x|$ is
A patient's temperature $T$ is modeled by $T(t) = 98.6 + 2 \sin(\frac{\pi t}{12})$, where $t$ is the time in hours. What is $T(6)$?

A hospital uses the function $C(x) = 2x + 5$ to calculate the cost of $x$ doses of a medication. Find the domain and range of this function and explain the practical meaning of these values in the context of the hospital's medication costs.

A medical researcher collects data on the effect of a new medication on blood pressure. The data is modeled by the function $f(x) = 120 - 10 \cdot 2^{-x}$, where $x$ is the number of hours after the medication is taken. Evaluate $f(2)$ and explain the practical meaning of this value in the context of the research.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students can identify the domain and range from various representations
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of the vertical line test in determining functions
Tip 3: Provide real-world applications to help students understand the practical significance of functions in healthcare and biology
